description:
Present a multi-field confirmation sheet so the user can review, adjust, and approve a complex configuration before you execute it. The user can edit individual field values inline, or reject the whole proposal with overall feedback typed below the form.

## When to Use
- Before an irreversible or outward-facing step that bundles several values, such as publishing or exporting plugin metadata (id, name, version, components, output path)
- Before applying a multi-field configuration where the user should be able to correct individual values instead of answering one question at a time
- When a workflow or skill explicitly requires confirming collected metadata through this tool

## When NOT to Use
- A single question or an either/or decision: use ask_user_question
- Approval of a single tool action: the tool confirmation flow already handles that
- Values you can derive with certainty and that are cheap to change later

## Outcome
The result reports either a confirmation with the final field values (including any user edits, listed under changedKeys) or a change request with the user's overall feedback. After a change request, revise the proposal and present an updated sheet before proceeding. Never proceed with the action while the latest sheet outcome is a change request.

## Guidelines
- Prefill every field with your best proposal; never present empty fields just to collect input
- Use stable snake_case or camelCase keys; keys are echoed back in the result
- Mark derived or locked values as readonly so the user sees them without editing them
- Use options for a closed set of valid choices, and multiline for long text such as descriptions
- Keep the sheet focused: one sheet per decision, at most 24 fields

title.description: Short title stating what the user is confirming, for example 'Publish plugin asset-tools 0.1.0'.

description.description: Optional context shown under the title: what happens after confirmation and anything the user should double-check.

confirmLabel.description: Optional label for the confirm button, for example 'Publish' or 'Export'. Defaults to a generic confirm label.

fields.description: Fields to review. Each field is prefilled with the proposed value and editable unless marked readonly.

key.description: Stable identifier echoed back in the result, for example 'version' or 'outputPath'.

label.description: Short display name for the field.

value.description: Proposed value shown to the user.

description.description: Optional one-line hint explaining the field or where the proposal came from.

multiline.description: Render a multi-line text area instead of a single-line input. Default false.

options.description: Optional closed set of valid values rendered as a dropdown. Include the proposed value.

readonly.description: Show the value without allowing edits. Edits submitted for readonly fields are ignored. Default false.
